subtasks.title: Database schema and migrations
acceptance_criteria: Postgres tables users, proposals, routine_logs exist with correct columns and types per spec
users.id is UUID with default gen_random_uuid()
proposals.content is JSONB
proposals.status is CHECK constraint with values draft, sent, accepted, rejected
routine_logs has started_at and finished_at timestamp columns
Foreign key from proposals.user_id to users.id with ON DELETE CASCADE
Migration files run idempotently via npm script

subtasks.title: Auth endpoints (register + login) with JWT middleware
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/register creates user and returns JWT token
POST /api/auth/login validates credentials and returns JWT token
Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (cost factor >= 10)
JWT contains user id and email, expires in 7 days
Auth middleware extracts and verifies JWT from Authorization header
Protected routes return 401 without valid token
Duplicate email registration returns 409

subtasks.title: Proposal CRUD routes
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/proposals returns all proposals for authenticated user
GET /api/proposals/:id returns single proposal owned by user (404 if not found, 403 if not owner)
POST /api/proposals creates a draft proposal with client_name, segment, service, estimated_value, deadline
PUT /api/proposals/:id updates proposal fields including content (JSONB)
PATCH /api/proposals/:id/status updates only the status field
DELETE /api/proposals/:id soft-deletes or hard-deletes proposal owned by user
All routes require authentication

subtasks.title: Groq proposal generation route
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/proposals/:id/generate calls Groq API with llama-3.3-70b model
Structured prompt includes client_name, segment, service, estimated_value, deadline
Groq response is parsed as JSON with sections: introduction, scope, investment, next_steps
Generated content is saved to proposals.content JSONB field
Returns the generated content in the response body
Handles Groq API errors gracefully with 502 status
Route requires authentication and proposal ownership

subtasks.title: Follow-up routine with node-cron
acceptance_criteria: node-cron job runs daily at 09:00 America/Sao_Paulo timezone
Routine queries proposals with status=sent and updated_at older than 3 days
For each matching proposal, calls Groq to generate personalized follow-up message
Each execution is logged in routine_logs with routine_name=follow-up-routine
Log includes triggered_by (cron or manual), status, proposals_affected count, and details JSONB
POST /api/routines/follow-up/trigger fires the routine manually (fire-and-forget, returns 202)
GET /api/routines/logs returns execution history ordered by started_at DESC

subtasks.title: Health check endpoint and Express app setup
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO string }
Express app has CORS configured for frontend origin
JSON body parser is configured
Error handling middleware returns structured error responses
Environment variables loaded from .env (PORT, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, GROQ_API_KEY)

subtasks.title: API client and auth hooks (frontend)
acceptance_criteria: Axios or fetch wrapper configured with base URL from env var
Auth interceptor attaches JWT token from localStorage to every request
useAuth hook exposes login, register, logout, user, isAuthenticated
Token is persisted in localStorage and cleared on logout
401 responses trigger automatic logout and redirect to /login

subtasks.title: Login and signup pages
acceptance_criteria: /login page has email and password fields with form validation
/register page has email, password, and confirm password fields
Successful login redirects to /proposals
Successful register redirects to /proposals
Error messages display for invalid credentials or duplicate email
Unauthenticated users are redirected to /login from protected routes

subtasks.title: Proposal creation form
acceptance_criteria: /proposals/new page has fields: client_name, segment, service, estimated_value, deadline
All fields have client-side validation (required, value >= 0, valid date)
Submit creates proposal via API and redirects to proposal result page
Loading state shown during submission
Error state shown if API call fails

subtasks.title: Proposal result and section editor
acceptance_criteria: /proposals/:id page displays generated proposal content by section
Generate button triggers AI generation and shows loading spinner
Each section (introduction, scope, investment, next_steps) is individually editable
Edits are saved via PUT /api/proposals/:id with updated content JSONB
Save button persists all section edits at once
Status badge shows current proposal status

subtasks.title: Saved proposals list page
acceptance_criteria: /proposals page lists all user proposals with client_name, status, created_at
Proposals with status=sent and updated_at > 3 days show pending follow-up indicator
Click on a proposal navigates to /proposals/:id
Status filter dropdown (all, draft, sent, accepted, rejected)
Empty state shown when no proposals exist

subtasks.title: Routines panel page
acceptance_criteria: /routines page displays execution history from GET /api/routines/logs
Each log entry shows routine_name, triggered_by, status, proposals_affected, started_at, finished_at
Manual trigger button calls POST /api/routines/follow-up/trigger
Trigger button shows confirmation before firing
Success toast after manual trigger

subtasks.title: PDF export with jsPDF
acceptance_criteria: Export PDF button on /proposals/:id page
PDF includes proposal header with client_name, date, and status
PDF renders all sections (introduction, scope, investment, next_steps) with proper formatting
PDF is generated client-side using jsPDF (no server round-trip)
Downloaded file is named proposal-{client_name}-{date}.pdf

subtasks.title: Docker and docker-compose setup
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile for backend (Node.js multi-stage build)
Dockerfile for frontend (Next.js standalone output)
docker-compose.yml defines services: frontend, backend, postgres
Postgres service uses named volume for data persistence
Backend depends_on postgres with healthcheck
Environment variables passed via .env file
docker compose up builds and runs all services successfully

assumptions: Groq API key is provided as environment variable GROQ_API_KEY
Postgres runs locally or via Docker; connection string in DATABASE_URL
JWT secret is a strong random string provided via JWT_SECRET env var
Frontend runs on port 3000, backend on port 3001
No email sending for follow-up — messages are stored in routine_logs.details for user to copy
No rate limiting on Groq calls (can be added later)
No password reset flow in MVP
No real-time updates — user refreshes to see routine results
Proposal delete is hard delete (no soft delete / trash)
Single-tenant — each user sees only their own proposals
